title: Nullable Variables (?) ❓
description: Learn how to safely tell Dart that a box might be empty.

slides.title: But I Want Null! 🥺
slides.content:
# The Question Mark

Dart forbids `null` by default. But what if a user is signing up, and their 'middle name' is totally optional?
We NEED a way to represent an empty box legally.

To tell Dart "this variable is ALLOWED to be empty", you add a Question Mark `?` right after the Data Type!

slides.title: Allowing the Empty Box 📦

quizData.question: Which variable declaration is allowed to safely store `null`?
quizData.explanation: Appending a ? to the type (like int?) signals to the compiler that this distinct variable is allowed to end up empty.

slides.title: The Compiler Knows All 👁️
slides.content:
## Forced Checking

Because `String?` is nullable, Dart's compiler is terrified. If you try to run `.length` on it, Dart throws a massive red warning:

*"Warning: The property 'length' can't be unconditionally accessed because the receiver can be 'null'."*

Dart FORCES you to write an `if` statement to check if the box is empty before you are allowed to touch it!

quizData.explanation: You manually check if (variable != null) to prove to the compiler that it is safe to use.

slides.title: See it in Action
slides.content: The compiler actually reads your `if` statements! Once the `if` proves it isn't null, Dart safely lets you run `.toUpperCase()`.
